mnemonics: Think of JDBL: Java's Dynamic Backbone (JSP, Servlets, Beans, JDBC & Libraries) for building web apps.
keyPoints: Servlets handle HTTP requests/responses on the server-side.
JavaServer Pages (JSP) simplify creating dynamic web content by mixing HTML and Java.
JavaBeans are reusable software components that follow specific naming conventions.
JDBC (Java Database Connectivity) is the API for connecting to and interacting with databases.
Frameworks like Spring build upon these core technologies to provide more structure and features.
commonMistakes: Confusing the difference between a Servlet (Java class) and a JSP (HTML-based page).
Not using PreparedStatement in JDBC, leaving code vulnerable to SQL injection attacks.
Forgetting to configure the web.xml deployment descriptor or understanding annotation-based configuration for Servlets.
